description: description
responsibilities: ZS Health Plan and Provider Team The Health Plan and Providers team at ZS partners with leading health plans and provider organizations to drive growth, improve member engagement, and enhance operational efficiency through technology and analytics. We deliver end-to-end solutions—from strategic advisory to implementation of advanced digital platforms and data-driven products—that address critical business challenges such as: Consumer Intelligence & Go-to-Market Strategy Value-Based Contracting & Population Health Management Interoperability & Data Integration Medicare STARs Rating Optimization Our team comprises experts in technology consulting, data engineering, AI/ML, and business analytics who collaborate closely with clients to design and implement scalable, innovative solutions. By leveraging deep healthcare domain knowledge and cutting-edge technology, we help clients navigate complex ecosystems and achieve measurable outcomes. Associate Principal – Business Technology in Health Plan & Provider The Associate Principal will lead the client engagements at a cross-office level encompassing the areas of digital transformation, analytics, business intelligence, information management, reporting and mobility; provide thought leadership in developing the firm’s services and offerings in Health Payers and Providers space and add leverage to the overall leadership span for the group in India. The person in this role will be responsible for growing the capabilities in the Business Technology group by developing and managing the existing client relationships and generating new business engagements. This person will need to fully understand the company and its service lines and actively assess/present ways to serve clients by developing solutions and offerings relevant to that client base. The successful candidate will work closely with ZS leadership; grow the practice by expanding client relationships and building trusted advisory relationships with executives at the company’s various accounts. Responsibilities: Lead large scale client delivery engagements in the Health Plan - Payers and Providers space while maintaining high Quality standards defined at ZS; Provide leadership to India teams to build innovative solutions leveraging advance digital and technology approaches Develop and manage client relationships on projects. Deeply understand client’s business challenges and provide thought leadership in addressing issues. Identify opportunities and contribute to the selling of additional work to new and existing clients; Provide technology solution framework and design support to the India team while solving a client problem Active involvement in pre-sales opportunities, building new assets and platforms that will support ZS strategic growth Understand the technology landscape in the broader healthcare industry and propose advancements to our roadmap Leverage experience in project management activities including project planning/estimation, scoping by ensuring delivery of quality outcomes within timelines and budgets while tightly managing project/space metrics such as delivery margin and utilization etc. Foster an innovative and inclusive team-oriented work environment; Remain current on new developments in advisory services capabilities and industry knowledge. Play an active role at the India leadership level supporting new India based initiatives. Lead the development & scaling of India Technology team Qualifications: Bachelor's/master's degree with specialization in Computer or IT, or other computer related disciplines; 18+ years of consulting-industry experience working on medium-large scale technology solution delivery engagements; Deep industry knowledge in US Healthcare with primary focus on Payers and Providers space; Extensive experience in creating enterprise scale data engineering pipelines, data-based decision-making, and quantitative analysis; Deep understanding of healthcare data i.e., claims, EHR, membership data, agency data etc. Exposure with complex, high volume, multi-dimensional data, as well as unstructured, structured, and streaming datasets Design and delivery of complex technology solutions by applying your deep skills to solve priority issues and help improve performance; Demonstrated experience with driving revenue within existing clients and in developing new prospects at the "C" level of our clients; Strong expertise in building enterprise solutions using diverse technology i.e., Big Data, Cloud, MDM etc. At least 2 years of hands-on experience on data platforms - Databricks or Snowflake; Involved in the selling process by writing proposals and giving product demos; Strong credentials and demonstrated success in IT strategy and Enterprise Architecture; Ability to lead a virtual team of consulting professionals across multiple projects; A strong work ethic; A willingness to travel to meet client needs; Executive-level presence and communication. How you’ll grow: Cross-functional skills development & custom learning pathways Milestone training programs aligned to career progression opportunities Internal mobility paths that empower growth via s-curves, individual contribution and role expansions Hybrid working model: ZS is committed to a Flexible and Connected way of working. ZSers are onsite at clients or ZS offices three days a week. Combined flexibility to work remotely two days a week is also available. The magic of ZS culture and innovation thrives in both planned and spontaneous face-to-face connections

description: description
responsibilities: Strategy & Operations Consultant The S&O Consultant collaborates with senior executives leading either a client account team, practice area, or industry vertical (collectively referred to as “spaces”). In this role, you will be a critical contributor to the space you support. You will work with leaders to prioritize and drive key space initiatives and create the structure and processes that enable the space to achieve its vision, strategy, and key milestones. S&O professionals are key connectors with broad visibility across the space, building collaborative relationships within the space and across ZS to enhance efficiency. This involves providing strategic and operational support across 8 dimensions: 1) Space Management & Operations, 2) Strategic Planning & Enablement, 3) Investment Budget Planning & Optimization, 4) ZS-Client Working Model Strategy and Management, 5) External Engagement & Thought Leadership, 6) People Development & Engagement, 7) Business Development/ Pipeline Strategy & Management, 8) Capability Building & Dissemination As an S&O Consultant you will have the unique opportunity to work at the intersection of strategy, leadership, and innovation, collaborating with senior executives and cross-functional teams to drive significant impact. You will help shape the future of the space you support, identifying and implementing solutions that streamline operations, foster growth, and maximize client success. Through your work, you will foster a culture of collaboration, accountability, and continuous improvement, ensuring that each space operates at its highest potential while delivering measurable results to both clients and ZS. What You Will Do Leverage problem solving skills and frameworks to develop solutions to business problems Optimize space operations to minimize leadership’s time spent on operational tasks Ensure timely and accurate responses to firm requests Contribute to strategic planning processes (annual and ongoing) Contribute to impact measurement and communication strategies Manage and provide insights on budgets and total annual spend Foster relationships with client procurement stakeholders and help ensure compliance with client requirements Develop reports on BD win rates and pipeline opportunities Create and synthesize space materials; prepare leadership level presentations Support space-level people strategy by keeping a pulse of team and provide ongoing insight to leadership Plan and run effective workshops, events, summits, client visits, etc. (virtual and in-person) Develop new processes to be leveraged across teams What You Will Bring BA required, MBA or other advanced degree preferred 5-8 years experience in professional services firm, consulting or strategy/operations experience preferred Strong consultative and problem-solving skills Effective oral and written communication skills that can influence senior-level decision makers Experience successfully managing multiple projects at once, including those with intersecting workstreams Experience and comfort working with multiple and senior stakeholders Understanding of consulting business models Understanding of systems and processes Empathy, adaptability and high personal impact Strong productivity and collaboration tools, including MS Office (PowerPoint, Outlook, Excel, Teams) High motivation, good work ethic, maturity and personal initiative Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach How you’ll grow: Cross-functional skills development & custom learning pathways Milestone training programs aligned to career progression opportunities Internal mobility paths that empower growth via s-curves, individual contribution and role expansions Fluency in English Client first mentality Intense work ethics Collaborative spirit and problem solving skill Hybrid working model: ZS is committed to a Flexible and Connected way of working. ZSers are onsite at clients or ZS offices three days a week. Combined flexibility to work remotely two days a week is also available. The magic of ZS culture and innovation thrives in both planned and spontaneous face-to-face connections

description: description
responsibilities: Finance Associate - Client Accounting (Billing) We are seeking for someone who will be resposnsible for Invoicing role. The position involves managing client invoicing, purchase orders, billing compliance, and financial reporting, requiring a bachelor’s degree in finance or related fields, 0-3 years of invoicing experience, proficiency in Microsoft Office, strong analytical skills, attention to detail, and effective communication. What You'll do: Work alongside Client Accounting/ Business Partnership teams to support project managers actively manage project financial health; Engage with project teams, colleagues and clients to identify and resolve issues related to project financial health; Support “in-flight” client projects. Provide reporting, trend analysis, compliance tracking, financial status and forecasts. Engage effectively with resources at all levels within ZS and at our clients, as appropriate Manage revenue recognition, completion estimates, write-offs and margins Ensure project financial transactions (billing, collection, time & expenses) are completed in a timely and accurate manner Assist with engagement startup (project setup) and shutdown activities (prepare final bill to client, close project codes etc.) Receive, interpret, and process billing instructions, following up with project teams for clarification as needed. Send invoices to clients through multiple channels (email, online, postal mail) as dictated by client. Manage client PO’s- receipt of PO’s, communication with project teams to assign to projects. Maintain electronic archive of all invoices and log of invoice details. What You'll Bring Bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, or business required, advanced degree welcome, with a strong record of academic achievement. 0-3 years of invoicing/billing experience. Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, Outlook), SAP experience desirable. Strong analytic and critical thinking abilities Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

Excellent interpersonal, oral, and written communication skills. Strong customer service orientation. Self-motivated and directed, with strong attention to detail. description: description
responsibilities: About the Expertise Centre – R&D Technologist The R&D Technologist Expertise Centre within ZS’s Drug Development practice partners with biopharma organizations to design and deliver innovative clinical data solutions that accelerate drug development and reduce risk from portfolio to patient. The team operates at the intersection of key ZS drug development verticals—including asset strategy, clinical trial design, clinical data management, statistical programming, regulatory, and risk-based quality. By combining deep clinical domain expertise, data standards, clinical programming, systems understanding, and consulting discipline, the team solves complex R&D challenges with speed, scale, and measurable impact. Director Expertise Center R&D Technologist – Clinical data solutions The Director – R&D Technologist (Clinical Data Solutions) provides strategic and scientific leadership across clinical data management, analytics, biostatistics, statistical programming, and clinical data standards. This role is central to the R&D Technologist Expertise Centre and focuses on designing and delivering innovative clinical data solutions across the drug development lifecycle—with a strong emphasis on clinical data standards, transformation, and analytics-driven decision-making. You will combine deep technical expertise, consulting leadership, and practice-building capabilities to serve as a trusted advisor to senior stakeholders, driving next-generation clinical data and analytics solutions that improve development outcomes and accelerate regulatory submissions What You’ll Do: Strategic & Client Leadership Provide strategic leadership across clinical data management, biometrics, and advanced analytics for end-to-end drug development programs Serve as ZS’s go-to leader for clinical data solutions, bringing a strong techno-functional perspective across clinical data flow and systems Lead complex client engagements spanning clinical data strategy, analytics enablement, and digital R&D transformation Facilitate senior-level workshops to align stakeholders on study design, data standards, analytics approaches, and operating models Clinical Data & Standards Leadership Bring deep expertise in clinical data standards (e.g., CDISC – CDASH, SDTM, ADaM, TLF) Define innovative approaches to clinical data collection, transformation, and analytics Integrate clinical programming, analytics, and data science into trial design, execution, and regulatory workflows Drive adoption of standardized, scalable, and future-ready clinical data models Technology & Innovation Apply a techno-functional lens across clinical systems, data platforms, and enterprise architectures Leverage AI/ML and GenAI approaches to transform clinical data processing and analytics Define future-state operating models and digital ecosystems for clinical data and analytics Delivery Excellence & Governance Oversee quality, scientific rigor, and compliance of deliverables, including analytics, data models, and regulatory outputs Ensure alignment with GxP standards, regulatory expectations, and industry best practices Act as a bridge between scientific, business, and engineering teams Team & Practice Building Lead and grow a high-performing clinical data and analytics team Mentor senior talent and establish standards for delivery excellence Contribute to capability building, accelerators, and new offerings within the Expertise Centre Support business development through solutioning, client pursuits, and long-term relationship building
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Experience & Domain Expertise 18+ years of experience in Life Sciences R&D, with strong expertise in clinical data management, biometrics, and analytics Deep understanding of the end-to-end drug development lifecycle Strong domain knowledge of clinical data, analytics, and digital R&D transformation Clinical Systems & Platforms Experience working with: EDC Platforms: Medidata Rave, Veeva EDC, Oracle InForm Clinical Systems: ePRO, laboratory systems, CTMS, eTMF, CDMS Clinical Data Repositories: SAS LSAF, Oracle LSH, eClinical Elluminate Metadata Repositories: Nurocor, Sycamore, Formedix Statistical Computing: SAS, SAS Viya, Domino Data Standards & Interoperability Strong expertise in: CDISC: CDASH, SDTM, ADaM, TLF Interoperability: FHIR, HL7, USDM Technical & Analytical Skills Working knowledge of R, Python, and SAS for clinical data transformation and analysis Exposure to AI/ML and GenAI-based solutions in clinical data and analytics Strong capability in business process modeling, data flow modeling, and solution architecture design Leadership & Consulting Skills Strong communication and stakeholder management skills with ability to influence senior leaders Proven experience in GxP-compliant, large-scale enterprise programs Ability to lead global, cross-functional, and multi-vendor teams Strong leadership, mentoring, and team-building capabilities Solid understanding of agile, product-led, and digital transformation delivery models

description: description
responsibilities: What You’ll Do: Build, Refine and Use ML Engineering platforms and components. Scaling machine learning algorithms to work on massive data sets and strict SLAs. Build and orchestrate model pipelines including feature engineering, inferencing and continuous model training. Implement ML Ops including model KPI measurements, tracking, model drift & model feedback loop. Write production-ready code that is easily testable, understood by other developers and accounts for edge cases and errors. Ensure highest quality of deliverables by following architecture/design guidelines, coding best practices, periodic design/code reviews. Write unit tests as well as higher level tests to handle expected edge cases and errors gracefully, as well as happy paths. Uses bug tracking, code review, version control and other tools to organize and deliver work. Participate in scrum calls and agile ceremonies, and effectively communicate work progress, issues and dependencies. Consistently contribute in researching & evaluating latest architecture patterns/technologies through rapid learning, conducting proof-of-concepts and creating prototype solutions. What You’ll Bring A master's or bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field from a top university. 1-3 years’ hands-on experience in ML development. Good understanding of the fundamentals of machine learning Strong programming expertise in Python, PySpark/Scala. Expertise in crafting ML Models for high performance and scalability. Experience in implementing feature engineering, inferencing pipelines, and real time model predictions. Experience in ML Ops to measure and track model performance, experience working with MLFlow Experience with Spark or other distributed computing frameworks. Experience in ML platforms like Sage maker, Kubeflow. Experience with pipeline orchestration tools such Airflow. Experience in deploying models to cloud services like AWS, Azure, GCP, Azure ML. Expertise in SQL, SQL DB's. Knowledgeable of core CS concepts such as common data structures and algorithms. Collaborate well with teams with different backgrounds / expertise / functions Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collabrative spirit and probelm-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: Knowledge Services Group Overview Knowledge Services group offers a broad spectrum of Business Research, Pre-Sales and other knowledge-based services to clients and internal stakeholders. Knowledge Services has emerged as one of ZS’s distinctive competitive advantages, and a key enabler for our consultants to have lasting impact while addressing their clients' sales & marketing issues. Our services are based on deep research, and we bring together a unique combination of Business Research, Life-Sciences and Clinical experiences. What You’ll Do Assess firm knowledge needs and requirements (short-term or long-term) Deliver targeted insights using internal knowledge and external sources Help the firm prepare materials for Summits and Conferences Support the creation and update of knowledge assets including client updates, competitive intelligence, industry research etc. Maintain, manage, and develop the firm’s knowledge infrastructure Share best practices and tactical knowledge across offices, practice areas and team Engage and build relationships with firm members and leaders What You’ll Bring A track record in research and knowledge services, ready to support diverse ZS practice areas and project teams. 48+ months of relevant knowledge services experience in IT/Consulting companies; An academic background in life sciences, biotechnology with PhD only. Experience in working on databases like Evaluate Pharma, Adis, Avention, Datamonitor, Nexis etc; Proficiency in computer applications, including MS Office, with an interest in Enterprise application/BI tools; Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

As a Software Engineer - Big Data/Java/Scala at JPMorgan Chase, you will join the Liquidity risk (LRI) team to design and implement the next generation buildout of a cloud native liquidity risk (LRI) management platform for JPMC.  The Liquidity risk technology organization aims to provide comprehensive solution to managing the firm’s liquidity risk and to meet our regulatory reporting obligations across 50+ markets.   The program will include strategic the buildout of advanced liquidity calculation engines, incorporate AI and ML into our liquidity risk processes, and bring digital first reporting capabilities. The target platform must process 40-60 million transactions and positions daily, calculate risk presented by the current actual as well as model-based what-if state of the market, build multidimensional picture of corporate risk profile, and provide ability to analyze it in real time
